name: Romagnol

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Romagnol (roma1328).

notes: Skeleton. Gallo-Italic (Romance, Emilian-Romagnol) variety of Italy written in the Latin alphabet. Grapheme map follows Italian-based conventions: c and g are affricates [tʃ]/[dʒ] by default with ch/gh for the plain velars, gn [ɲ], gl [ʎ], plus accented vowels distinguishing open/close and long qualities (è [ɛ], ò [ɔ], ê/ô long). Romagnol's rich system of vowel length and quality distinctions is only partly written; contextual (before e/i) softening of c/g and the full length inventory are not fully recoverable and are left unencoded, so a real PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Romagnol (roma1328)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
